san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting on March 25, 2026 provided information about how residents can watch the proceedings online or on cable TV. The agenda focused on making the meeting accessible to all residents, including those who speak languages other than English.
keyTopics: Live translations available in over 50 languages
Multiple ways to watch: Cable Channel 26, YouTube, or Zoom
Phone dial-in option available for residents without internet
Meeting streaming on City of San José website

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 25, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council's zoning hearing that was supposed to happen on March 25, 2026 has been cancelled. The next zoning hearing will take place instead on April 8, 2026. If you had plans to attend or present something at the March hearing, you'll need to wait until mid-April.
keyTopics: March 25 zoning hearing cancelled
Rescheduled to April 8, 2026
Administrative zoning decisions delayed

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund affordable housing and community development projects in the city. The commission approved minutes from their previous meeting and heard presentations from organizations seeking funding to support local housing and human services programs.
keyTopics: Federal grant funding (CDBG) applications for affordable housing projects
Community development funding opportunities
Human services program support
February meeting minutes approval

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council focused on helping local businesses grow and succeed through several new programs. They discussed ways to reach out to business owners about licenses, plans to encourage shopping locally during summer events, and financial incentives to attract new retail stores and startups to Cupertino.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program to help shop owners get into compliance
Outreach efforts targeting shopping centers and local businesses
Summer campaign to encourage residents to shop at local stores
City partnerships with private companies to sponsor community events
Tax breaks and incentives to attract new retail and startup companies

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 24, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on March 24, 2026, where they postponed several agenda items for later discussion. The council also approved a plan to remove overgrown weeds throughout the city as part of the county's weed abatement program, and opened public comment on how federal housing grant money will be spent in the coming year.
keyTopics: Multiple agenda items postponed to future meetings
Weed removal program authorization for 2026
Federal housing grant spending plan (FY 2026/27) open for public input

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a meeting on March 23, 2026 where they honored Women's History Month with a proclamation. They approved an agreement with the American Red Cross to use city facilities during disasters, and authorized spending nearly $93,400 per year for email security software for the city.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Emergency shelter partnership with American Red Cross
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late packet report added to agenda
